Defines the PeekLayer structure and functions for creating, managing, and animating peek layers in a UI.

# Purpose
The code defines a C header file for managing a graphical user interface component called a `PeekLayer`. This component is designed to display an icon and associated text fields, such as a number, title, and subtitle, in a visually appealing manner. The `PeekLayer` provides functionality to animate the display of these elements, allowing for unfolding and scaling animations. The header file includes definitions for creating, initializing, and destroying `PeekLayer` instances, as well as functions to set various properties such as the frame, icon, text fields, background color, and animation duration.

The `PeekLayer` structure is the central component, containing fields for managing the graphical elements and their properties, such as `bg_color`, `kino_layer`, and `text_buffer`. The file also defines constants for animation durations and text length limits. The functions provided allow for detailed customization of the `PeekLayer`, including setting the text and font for each text field, adjusting the icon's position, and controlling the visibility of text fields. This header file is intended to be included in other C source files that require the use of the `PeekLayer` component, providing a public API for its manipulation and display.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/graphics/graphics.h`
- `applib/ui/layer.h`
- `applib/ui/kino/kino_layer.h`
- `applib/ui/text_layer.h`
- `services/common/evented_timer.h`
- `services/normal/timeline/timeline_resources.h`
- `services/normal/timeline/item.h`


# Data Structures

---
### PeekTextLayer
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``text_layer``: A `TextLayer` object that manages the display of text.
    - ``text_buffer``: A character array with a maximum length defined by `MAX_PEEK_LAYER_TEXT_LEN` to store text content.
- **Description**: Defines a structure that combines a `TextLayer` for text display and a buffer to hold the text content, facilitating the management and rendering of text within a graphical user interface.


---
### PeekLayer
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - `layer`: A `Layer` object that represents the base graphical layer.
    - `bg_color`: A `GColor` object that specifies the background color.
    - `kino_layer`: A `KinoLayer` object for additional graphical elements.
    - `number`: A `PeekTextLayer` object for displaying a number.
    - `title`: A `PeekTextLayer` object for displaying a title.
    - `subtitle`: A `PeekTextLayer` object for displaying a subtitle.
    - `res_info`: An `AppResourceInfo` object that contains resource information.
    - `hidden_fields_timer`: An `EventedTimerID` object for managing hidden fields timing.
    - `icon_offset_y`: An `int16_t` value for vertical offset of the icon.
    - `subtitle_margin`: An `int16_t` value for the margin between subtitle and title.
    - `dot_diameter`: A `uint8_t` value for the diameter of a dot.
    - `show_dot`: A `bool` value indicating whether to show a dot.
- **Description**: Represents a graphical layer that unfolds an icon to provide context or a preview of content that will appear on the screen. It includes various text layers for displaying numbers, titles, and subtitles, as well as properties for managing animations and visual elements such as background color and icon positioning.


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### peek\_layer\_create<!-- {{#callable_declaration:peek_layer_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.h#L64>)

Creates a new peek layer with a specified frame.
- **Description**: Use this function to create a new `PeekLayer` object with a given frame. This function allocates memory for the `PeekLayer` and initializes it with the specified frame. Ensure that the frame provided is valid and that there is sufficient memory available for allocation. The caller is responsible for managing the memory of the created `PeekLayer`, including its eventual destruction using `peek_layer_destroy` to prevent memory leaks.
- **Inputs**:
    - `frame`: A `GRect` structure that specifies the frame for the new peek layer. The frame must define a valid rectangular area.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to the newly created `PeekLayer` if successful, or `NULL` if memory allocation fails.
- **See Also**: [`peek_layer_create`](<peek_layer.c.md#peek_layer_create>)  (Implementation)


---
### peek\_layer\_set\_duration<!-- {{#callable_declaration:peek_layer_set_duration}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.h#L94>)

Sets the duration of the primed animation in milliseconds.
- **Description**: Use this function to specify the duration for the animation that is prepared to play on the `PeekLayer`. This function must be called before playing the animation to ensure the desired timing is applied. The duration is set in milliseconds and affects how long the animation will take to complete once it is triggered.
- **Inputs**:
    - `peek_layer`: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure. Must not be null. The function modifies the animation duration of this `PeekLayer`.
    - `duration`: The duration of the animation in milliseconds. Must be a non-negative integer.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`peek_layer_set_duration`](<peek_layer.c.md#peek_layer_set_duration>)  (Implementation)


---
### peek\_layer\_create\_play\_animation<!-- {{#callable_declaration:peek_layer_create_play_animation}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.h#L103>)

Creates and returns an animation for a peek layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to create an animation for a given peek layer. This function makes the peek layer visible and returns an animation object that can be used to play the animation. Ensure that the `peek_layer` is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `peek_layer`: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure. Must not be null. The function assumes the peek layer is initialized and ready for animation creation.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to an `ImmutableAnimation` object representing the created animation for the peek layer.
- **See Also**: [`peek_layer_create_play_animation`](<peek_layer.c.md#peek_layer_create_play_animation>)  (Implementation)


---
### peek\_layer\_create\_play\_section\_animation<!-- {{#callable_declaration:peek_layer_create_play_section_animation}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.h#L106>)

Creates an animation for a section of a peek layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to create an animation for a specific section of a peek layer, defined by the elapsed time range. This function makes the peek layer visible and returns an animation object that represents the specified section. Ensure that the `peek_layer` is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `peek_layer`: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` object. Must not be null and must be initialized before use. The function will make this layer visible.
    - `from_elapsed_ms`: The starting point of the animation section in milliseconds. Must be a non-negative integer.
    - `to_elapsed_ms`: The ending point of the animation section in milliseconds. Must be greater than or equal to `from_elapsed_ms`.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to an `ImmutableAnimation` object representing the created animation section. The caller is responsible for managing the lifecycle of the returned animation object.
- **See Also**: [`peek_layer_create_play_section_animation`](<peek_layer.c.md#peek_layer_create_play_section_animation>)  (Implementation)
